title: Une machine, deux pays, un grand conflit
gist: One machine — made only by the Dutch company ASML — produces the world's most advanced computer chips, the kind that power the best AI. Since 2019 the U.S. has blocked its sale to China, and now a U.S. official claims one may have reached China anyway. ASML flatly denies it and the Dutch government backs them. Underneath sits a bigger US–Europe disagreement over China, China's own effort to build such a machine, and a proposed U.S. law the Dutch dislike.

body.fr: Il y a une machine très {{spéciale|special}} et très {{importante|important}}.

bd.meaning: There is a very special and very important machine.
chunks: there is a machine (il y a = there is)
very special and very important — both take -e to match machine (feminine)
bd.key: Adjective agrees with its noun. machine is feminine, so spéciale and importante both end in -e.

bd.meaning: This machine makes the best computer chips.
chunks: this machine makes (fabriquer = to make / manufacture)
the best chips (une puce = a chip)
bd.key: meilleur = 'best / better'. les meilleures puces = 'the best chips' — meilleures agrees (feminine plural).

body.fr: Ces puces sont {{importantes|important}} pour l'{{intelligence artificielle (l'IA)|artificial intelligence (AI)}}.

bd.meaning: These chips are important for artificial intelligence.
chunks: these chips are important — importantes agrees (feminine plural)
bd.key: pour = 'for'. pour l'IA = 'for AI' — the same little word as in pour aider ('to help').

body.fr: Une {{seule|single / only}} {{entreprise|company}} fabrique cette machine : ASML.

bd.meaning: Only one company makes this machine: ASML.
chunks: only one / a single company (seul = only / single)
bd.key: seul before the noun = 'only / single'. une seule entreprise = 'only one company'.

bd.meaning: ASML is a company from the Netherlands.
chunks: from the Netherlands (les Pays-Bas = the Netherlands)
bd.key: des = de + les. des Pays-Bas = 'of / from the Netherlands' — the country's name itself is plural (les Pays-Bas).

body.fr: Depuis 2019, les États-Unis {{interdisent|forbid}} de vendre cette machine à la Chine.

bd.meaning: Since 2019, the United States has forbidden selling this machine to China.
chunks: the United States forbid (interdire = to forbid)
de vendre cette machine à la Chine
to sell this machine to China (interdire de + plain verb)
bd.key: depuis = 'since / for'. French uses the present with depuis where English says 'has': depuis 2019, ils interdisent = 'since 2019 they have forbidden'.

body.fr: Mais aujourd'hui, un {{ministre|minister}} américain dit : peut-être qu'une machine est {{allée|went (from aller)}} en Chine.

bd.meaning: But today, a U.S. minister says: maybe a machine went to China.
chunks: a U.S. minister says (américain comes after the noun)
bd.key: Past with être, not avoir. est allée = 'went': a few verbs (like aller) use être in the past, and then the verb agrees — allée has an extra -e for machine (feminine).
c'est = 'it is / that's'. c'est impossible = 'it's impossible' — the everyday set phrase.

body.fr: L'entreprise dit : nous {{savons|know}} où sont toutes nos machines.

bd.meaning: The company says: we know where all our machines are.
chunks: we know (savoir = to know a fact)
where all our machines are (toutes agrees: feminine plural)
bd.key: savoir = 'to know' (a fact). nous savons = 'we know'. French says où sont ('where are') with no extra word.

chunks: no machine / not one machine (aucun / aucune = none)
is not in China — aucune… ne is a negative pair
bd.key: aucune … ne = 'no / not any'. Like ne…pas, it comes in two parts: aucune machine n'est = 'no machine is'.

body.fr: Le gouvernement des Pays-Bas est {{d'accord|in agreement}}.

bd.key: être d'accord = 'to agree'. French says 'is in agreement': est d'accord.

bd.meaning: It says: we follow the rules.
chunks: we follow / respect the rules (respecter = to respect / obey)
bd.key: -ons = the 'we' ending. nous respectons, nous savons — verbs for nous ('we') end in -ons.

body.fr: Mais {{derrière|behind}} ce problème, il y a un plus grand {{conflit|conflict}}.

bd.meaning: But behind this problem, there is a bigger conflict.
chunks: il y a un plus grand conflit
there is a bigger conflict (plus grand = bigger)
bd.key: plus grand = 'bigger'. plus + adjective makes the comparison: plus grand = 'bigger', like plus cher ('more expensive').

body.fr: Les États-Unis et l'Europe ne sont pas toujours d'accord sur la Chine.

bd.meaning: The United States and Europe don't always agree about China.
chunks: don't always agree — ne…pas wraps sont
bd.key: Negation sandwich: ne … pas. It still wraps the verb with toujours tucked inside: ne sont pas toujours = 'are not always'.

body.fr: La Chine veut aussi fabriquer sa {{propre|own}} machine.

bd.meaning: China also wants to make its own machine.
chunks: China also wants to make (vouloir + plain verb)
its own machine (propre before the noun = 'own')
bd.key: propre before the noun = 'own'. sa propre machine = 'its own machine'. (After the noun, propre means 'clean' — position changes the meaning!)

body.fr: Mais c'est très difficile : ça peut prendre {{des années|years}}.

bd.meaning: But it's very difficult: it can take years.
chunks: it can take years (ça = that / it; des années = years)
bd.key: ça peut + plain verb. ça peut prendre = 'it can take' — ça ('that/it') + peut (can) + the plain verb prendre.

body.fr: Les États-Unis veulent une {{nouvelle loi|new law}} plus stricte.

bd.meaning: The United States wants a new, stricter law.
chunks: the United States want (vouloir = to want)
a new, stricter law (nouvelle before the noun; plus stricte = stricter)
bd.key: nouveau / nouvelle goes BEFORE the noun. une nouvelle loi = 'a new law' — and it agrees: nouvelle for the feminine loi.

body.fr: Mais les Pays-Bas ne sont pas {{contents|happy}}.

bd.meaning: But the Netherlands is not happy.
chunks: are not happy — ne…pas wraps sont; contents takes -s (plural)
bd.key: Negation sandwich: ne … pas. ne sont pas contents = 'are not happy'.

body.fr: C'est une {{petite bataille|small battle}} dans une grande {{guerre|war}} pour la technologie.

bd.meaning: It's a small battle in a big war over technology.
chunks: it's a small battle (petite before the noun)
in a big war (grande before the noun)
bd.key: Size adjectives go BEFORE the noun. petite bataille, grande guerre — petit and grand sit in front, and both agree (feminine).

grammar.fr: une machine est allée en Chine
grammar.en: Some verbs form the past with être, not avoir. aller is one: est allée = 'went'. With être the past word agrees with the subject — allée gains an extra -e for the feminine machine. (Contrast a gagné, which uses avoir and doesn't agree.)

grammar.fr: veut fabriquer / ça peut prendre / veulent une loi
grammar.en: helper + plain verb. veut, peut, veulent carry the subject; the next verb stays plain. Your pouvoir / vouloir / devoir family.

grammar.fr: une seule entreprise / nouvelle loi / plus grand conflit / petite bataille
grammar.en: a small set of adjectives sit BEFORE the noun (grand, petit, nouveau, seul, propre…) and still agree: seule, nouvelle, petite.

quiz.q: Pourquoi cette machine est très importante ?
quiz.a: Parce qu'elle fabrique les meilleures puces, et ces puces sont importantes pour l'IA. (Because it makes the best chips, and these chips are important for AI.)

flag: Compressed very hard from a long geopolitical piece into a plain A1 through-line — a neutral retelling, not a view on the export-control politics. I dropped the historical opening (Peter the Great, A.Q. Khan), the DUV-vs-EUV and 'multi-patterning' detail, the named people (Lutnick, Sjoerdsma) and ASML's exact count of 340 machines, the Pax Silica alliance, the MATCH Act specifics, the Nvidia chips, and the Nexperia dispute. Word choices to flag: une puce is the normal French word for a computer chip (it also means 'flea' — context makes it clear); interdire de vendre ('forbid to sell') is my plain stand-in for formal contrôles à l'exportation ('export controls'); and I used les Pays-Bas ('the Netherlands') rather than the adjective néerlandais to keep it transparent. The est allée agreement is correct, but être-verbs + agreement is exactly the kind of thing worth double-checking as you advance.
